response.justification: Candidate has strong general engineering experience and notable geospatial exposure via GISCollective, but the role's primary requirement — modern C++ in commercial/open-source environments, or equivalent Rust — is essentially absent. C/C++ appears only briefly at National Instruments around 2000-2001 and a bachelor thesis; the modern stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. No Rust experience and no navigation/routing systems background. DLang familiarity and Linux fluency are partial proxies but do not substitute for production-grade modern C++ on a low-latency tiling pipeline.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Open Green Map)
Long Linux and systems-level background, including low-latency DLang services
Strong testing/TDD discipline and full-stack adaptability
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ experience; C/C++ stint is ~25 years old and brief
No Rust experience as the acceptable alternative
No navigation guidance / routing tile pipeline background
